title: Source Scorecards
description: Persisted source scorecard format. Combines the source-scorer sub-agent's judgment output (reliability, relevance, bias_assessment, overall_quality, optional content_summary/authors/date) with Python-coordinator-attached metadata (title, snippet, content_extract, items). Downstream sub-agents — evidence-extractor, synthesizer, self-auditor — read this combined format. The scorer itself emits only the narrower source-scorer-output.schema.json; the coordinator re-attaches content_extract/title/snippet from its own copy of the input, so the scorer never has to transcribe long article bodies back through the LLM.

source_scorecard.description: Self-contained judgment about one source plus the metadata needed to cite and quote it downstream. Fields split into two groups: scorer-emitted (reliability, relevance, bias_assessment, overall_quality, optional content_summary/authors/date) and Python-coordinator-attached (title, snippet, content_extract, items). The coordinator joins them after the scorer's LLM call.

title.description: Title of the source. Attached by the Python coordinator from the original search result — NOT emitted by the scorer sub-agent.

snippet.description: Snippet from the source. Attached by the Python coordinator from the original search result — NOT emitted by the scorer sub-agent.

content_extract.description: The fetched article body (via trafilatura for HTML, pypdf for PDFs) that was handed to the scorer. Attached by the Python coordinator from its own copy — NOT emitted by the scorer sub-agent. Preserved verbatim so downstream sub-agents (evidence-extractor, synthesizer) can quote and cite without re-fetching. No length truncation.

content_summary.description: Neutral short description of what the source says. Denormalized onto the scorecard so downstream artifacts (reading list, evidence packets) can carry it forward without joining back.

authors.description: Author line (names, institutions, or publisher) — denormalized for downstream use.

date.description: Publication or last-updated date — denormalized for downstream use.

items.description: IDs of the claims or queries this scorecard was produced in support of. Attached by the Python coordinator — NOT emitted by the scorer sub-agent.

relevance.description: How directly this addresses the research item.

overall_quality.description: Summary quality rating derived from the three components.
